subject: Geology
title: Regional Metamorphism
description: A fast breakdown of Regional Metamorphism for builders.

hook.text: When continents collide, whole mountain ranges get squeezed and cooked. The change spans hundreds of kilometers.

buildup.text: Regional metamorphism happens over large areas, driven by both intense pressure and heat from tectonic forces.

discovery.text: It produces foliated rocks like slate, schist, and gneiss, where minerals align in parallel layers or bands.

twist.text: Metamorphic grade increases with depth: shale becomes slate, then phyllite, then schist, then gneiss.

climax.text: Regional metamorphism is the signature of mountain building. Wherever you find schist, continents once collided.

punchline.text: Colliding continents rewrite rock at depth.

quiz.question: What causes regional metamorphism?
options: Tectonic forces producing both heat and pressure over large areas
